title: Senior Backend Engineer, Consumer Product
description:
flex is a growth\-stage, nyc headquartered fintech company that is creating the best rent payment experience. it’s hard to believe that it’s 2026 and paying rent on time is expensive, inflexible, and difficult. we’re here to change that! flex enables our users to pay rent throughout the month on a schedule that better fits their finances and budget. our mission is to empower as many renters as possible with flexibility over their most significant recurring expense. after deliberately keeping a stealth profile as we built up unprecedented investor support and an enthusiastic user base, we are looking for motivated individuals to help us keep our mission growing. will you be a part of the team?

  


**about the role**



flex is on a mission to change the way people pay for the things that matter most — starting with rent. as a senior engineer on our consumer product engineering team, you'll be part of the orchestration layer — a small, focused team responsible for the app\-friendly apis that connect our mobile and web apps to flex's core platform services. you'll design, develop, test, and scale the apis that sit at the heart of our customer experience, abstracting the complexity of rent payment processing into seamless, reliable interactions for our users.



this is a role for a product\-minded backend engineer who stays grounded in the experience their code enables. you'll work in close partnership with product, design, and engineering peers to solve our users' most pressing problems around rent payment, shipping changes in an agile cadence that favors small, frequent iterations. you'll lead scoping, design, and implementation for the apis you own and drive production rollouts to completion with a high degree of independence.



success here looks like building apis that make complex things feel simple, shipping high\-quality features on time, and raising the bar for the engineers around you. you'll bring both technical rigor and genuine product curiosity to a collaborative, fast\-moving team that cares deeply about what it builds and who it builds it for.


**what you'll do**


* design, develop, test, and scale app\-friendly service apis consumed by flex's mobile and web applications
* own end\-to\-end delivery of api features — from scoping and technical design through implementation, testing, deployment, and production operation to sla
* partner closely with product and design to shape requirements, pressure\-test specs, and deliver consumer experiences that simplify the complexity of rent payment for our customers
* lead scoping and technical design for the apis and orchestration services you own, driving rollouts to completion with minimal oversight
* build robust, fault\-tolerant integrations between consumer\-facing apis and core platform services, ensuring reliability and performance at scale
* write elegant, well\-tested java code and provide constructive code reviews that elevate team standards
* integrate ai\-assisted development tools into your daily workflow and mentor teammates on responsible, effective usage
* collaborate across engineering, product, and business stakeholders to align on scope, set clear expectations, and communicate progress proactively


**what you'll bring**


* 6\+ years of software engineering experience, including 4\+ years working in java
* proficiency in java frameworks and tooling used at flex: spring (core/web/boot), gradle, junit, and jvm tuning (memory, performance, gc)
* experience designing and building consumer\-facing products — you understand what it means to ship for real users at scale
* strong foundation in service\-oriented architecture, rest apis, message queues, and scalable distributed systems
* hands\-on experience with aws services (eks, aurora rds, elasticache, dynamodb) and containerization
* experience building and maintaining data pipelines and working with big data platforms such as snowflake
* proficiency with ci/cd systems (preferably github actions), git, and observability/monitoring tools such as datadog
* familiarity with infrastructure as code tools (cdk, terraform) and a track record of writing clean, maintainable, production\-grade code
* comfort working with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders, with strong written and verbal communication skills
* familiarity with ai\-assisted development tools and a thoughtful approach to evaluating their output for correctness, security, and alignment with team standards


flex takes a market\-based approach to pay, and compensation may vary depending on your primary work location. work locations are categorized into one of three tiers based on a cost of labor index for that geographic area. the successful candidate’s starting pay will be commensurate with their experience, qualifications, and flex’s internal leveling guidelines and benchmarks.
* **tier a (nyc/sf/seattle):** $172,000 \- $212,000 usd
* **tier b:** $154,800 \- $193,500 usd
* **tier c:** $146,200 \- $182,740 usd
### **life at flex**



we understand that it takes a diverse team of highly intelligent, curious, determined, empathetic, and self aware people to grow a successful company. our hq is located in new york city, but we have employees located throughout the us, australia, canada and south america. we are growing quickly, but deliberately, with a focus on building an inclusive culture. our dynamic team has incredible perspectives to share, just as we know you do, and we take great pride in being an equal opportunity workplace.


**offices**



roles posted in new york, san francisco, and salt lake city are hybrid positions with on\-site expectations of 2\-3 days per week in our local offices. for candidates outside of these areas, you may be eligible for our relocation assistance program.


**benefits**



for full\-time u.s. employees we offer:


* competitive medical, dental, and vision
* company equity
* 401(k) plan with company match
* unlimited paid time off \+ 13 company paid holidays
* parental leave
* flex cares program: non\-profit company match \+ pet adoption coverage
* free flex subscription


 for full\-time non\-u.s. employees, we offer:


* competitive compensation \+ company equity
* unlimited pto

title: Sr. Professional Services Engineer Consultant
description:
**the team \+ the role**
------------------------



pendo's professional services team is part of the broader strategy \& transformation organization. the team helps customers transform how they operate, not just how they use pendo, through technical and strategic consulting. this work spans complex technical engagements, including system migrations, integrations, and data work, as well as advisory support that helps customers evolve their processes, teams, and ways of working.



as a sr. professional services engineer consultant, you will own the design and delivery of complex technical engagements. you will work at the intersection of product, data, and engineering, extending pendo's capabilities through custom integrations, data pipelines, apis, and technical solutions that connect pendo to customers' broader ecosystems. you will partner with consultants and customer engineers to deliver outcome\-driven engagements that accelerate time to value and enable long\-term customer success.



this role is based in raleigh, san francisco or remote


**what this looks like day\-to\-day**
-------------------------------------


* **engagement design and scoping:** partner with account directors and customer engineers to qualify professional services opportunities and distinguish transformation gaps from tooling gaps. lead scoping conversations with customer stakeholders, define the problem, validate appetite, design engagement structures, and author statements of work that are commercially sound and delivery\-ready.
* **custom integrations and data solutions:** design and build technical solutions that extend pendo's capabilities and integrate with customer systems. write production\-quality code, build apis, design data pipelines, and implement etl processes across data warehouses, analytics platforms, bi tools, and emerging ai/ml systems.
* **technical project delivery:** own multiple complex technical projects end\-to\-end, including data engineering and system integration work. lead execution, troubleshoot issues, provide technical leadership on escalated engagements, and ensure delivery aligns to customer outcomes, scope, and timeline.
* **data and architecture consulting:** advise customers on data architecture, integration patterns, and long\-term technical strategy. support instrumentation design for advanced analytics and ai/ml use cases, and guide customers through technical transformations involving data infrastructure and system design.
* **enablement and reusability:** build reusable code libraries, frameworks, and integration patterns that scale across the professional services team. mentor consultants on data flows, api usage, debugging approaches, and technical problem\-solving to raise the overall capability of the organization.
* **practice impact and field intelligence:** identify patterns across engagements and turn them into reusable playbooks, documentation, and delivery approaches. share field intelligence with product and cross\-functional teams so customer transformation challenges inform how pendo supports adoption and long\-term value.

**who you are**
---------------



beyond the qualifications, we hire through a specific lens. these aren't buzzwords; they're the things we'll actually look for in how you talk about your work.



you're a builder, not a maintainer.



you're most energized when there isn't a clear path yet, and you get to define it. you don't wait for direction; you identify gaps, shape solutions, and drive them forward. at pendo, great sr. pse consultants don't just follow instructions; they operate as strategic advisors, influencing decisions, guiding stakeholders, and elevating how we work.



you're ai\-curious \- genuinely.



you're not using ai tools occasionally. you're rewiring how you work around them. you're faster, sharper, and more prolific because of it, and you bring that energy to everything — how you approach your work, how you prep, how you communicate, how you think. we want someone who sees ai as a multiplier, not a shortcut.


**must\-haves**
---------------


* 7\+ years of experience in a customer\-facing technical role within b2b saas, with hands\-on experience writing custom code and building integrations.
* strong proficiency in javascript, python, or node.js, with the ability to write production\-quality backend code.
* deep experience with apis, data pipelines, integration patterns, etl processes, and data modeling.
* sql proficiency and strong data modeling expertise.
* experience working with rest apis, webhooks, and event\-driven architectures.
* demonstrated ability to translate complex data concepts into business value for both technical and business stakeholders.
* experience leading technical transformation initiatives and data infrastructure projects.

**nice\-to\-haves**
-------------------


* bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, data science, or a related field.
* application development experience with node.js, python, or a related language or framework.
* experience with the modern data stack, such as segment, snowflake, bigquery, dbt, or fivetran.
* ai/ml integration experience, including feature engineering and mcp implementations.
* background in data engineering, integration consulting, or technical services focused on custom development.

**about pendo**
---------------



pendo was founded in 2013 by former product managers, who combined their heads and hearts to build something they wanted but never had as product managers: a simple way to understand and attack what truly drives product success. our mission is to improve society's experience with software. come join one of the fastest\-growing startups, supported by best\-in\-class institutions like battery ventures, salesforce ventures, spark capital and meritech.


**pendo core values:** bias to act, hone your craft, the team is pendo, and maniacal focus.


**location:** san francisco, raleigh or remote


**compensation:** the expected salary range for this role to be performed in san francisco $145,400 \- $175,000, raleigh or remote: $132,200 \- $159,0000


**benefits:** highly competitive, employer\-heavy coverage including $0 premium options, strong 401(k) match, equity, and flexible time off.


**eeoc:** we are an equal opportunity employer and believe having diverse teams where everyone brings their whole self to pendo is key to our success. we welcome all people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives.


**accessibility:** pendo is committed to working with, and providing access and reasonable accommodation to, applicants with mental and/or physical disabilities. if you think you may require an accommodation for any part of the recruitment process, please send a request to: accommodation@pendo.io. all requests for accommodations are treated discreetly and confidentially, as practical and permitted by law.

title: Senior BI Front End Architect
description:
**job description:**


looking for a rewarding career? johnson brothers is a leading family\-owned distributor of wine, spirits, and beer, delivering exceptional service since 1953\. we proudly represent the world’s top beverage brands and are committed to excellence, innovation, and growth. johnson brothers offers exciting opportunities in a variety of areas. join our passionate team and help shape the future of the beverage industry!

**role summary**


the senior bi front end architect designs and implements our enterprise power bi and microsoft fabric platform. you will consolidate multiple reporting environments into a single governed ecosystem, elevate ux/ui through standardized design, and optimize performance across all devices. while focused on front\-end architecture, you'll apply deep data acquisition knowledge to ensure semantic models accurately reflect business data from source systems. this individual contributor role actively coaches bi developers to uplift team capabilities.

**primary responsibilities**

**integration \& optimization**

* consolidate existing reporting environments into a cohesive **power bi \+ fabric** platform (workspaces, semantic models, and apps).
* establish front‑end governance: workspace taxonomy, naming standards, deployment pipelines, and versioning.
* define and enforce the **thin report over certified semantic models** pattern.
* act as the primary liaison between the bi team and data engineering, providing requirements for data models and pipelines based on business reporting needs.
* partner with business stakeholders and analytics teams to identify, prototype, and integrate ai/ml insights into reporting solutions.

**ux/ui evolution**

* create and maintain a **design system** for power bi (themes, layout patterns, typography, color, accessibility).
* standardize navigation (app structure, report landing pages, bookmarks) and **mobile‑first layouts**.
* build reusable visual templates and measure libraries to accelerate consistent delivery.

**performance optimization**

* own report performance: **dax optimization, model sizing, aggregation/hybrid tables, incremental refresh**, and visual/query reduction.
* monitor and tune **fabric/power bi capacity** using capacity metrics; set slis/slos for open time and interaction latency.
* establish performance test gates in ci/cd (performance analyzer baselines, dataset refresh thresholds).

**mentorship \& enablement**

* coach bi teams on dax patterns, modeling, and ux standards; run design and code reviews to ensure consistency across the organization.
* document standards, anti‑patterns, and “cookbook” guidance for repeatable delivery.

**quality \& governance**

* partner with data engineering to align **security (rls/ols), lineage, and metadata**.
* implement bi deployment processes including alm toolkit, git, and change management.

**technical stack \& tools**

* **core:** microsoft fabric, power bi desktop/service, dax, power query (m), **tabular editor,** **alm toolkit**, dax studio.
* **data/integration:** fabric pipelines \& dataflows gen2, azure sql/synapse, onelake, power bi deployment pipelines, azure devops.
* **monitoring \& ops:** power bi/ fabric **capacity metrics**, usage metrics, purview (lineage), azure monitor/log analytics.
* **ux/visualization:** power bi themes (json), custom visuals governance, accessibility guidelines (wcag), mobile layout optimizer.

**experience required**

* **8\+ years** in bi/analytics with **4\+ years** leading power bi front‑end architecture at enterprise scale.
* proven ability to collaborate with data engineering teams and a strong understanding of end\-to\-end data acquisition flows from source systems (e.g., erp, crm)
* demonstrated success consolidating disparate bi estates into a **unified fabric/power bi** platform.
* deep expertise in **dax performance tuning, model design, ux patterns, and capacity optimization**.
* hands‑on ci/cd for bi (git, deployment pipelines, alm toolkit) and production operations (refresh slas, incident handling).

**stakeholder interaction**

* translate executive and functional requirements into **task‑focused, decision‑ready** reporting.
* collaborate with data engineering, security, and governance to align modeling, lineage, and access.
* conduct design reviews, office hours, and enablement sessions; serve as a trusted advisor to business leaders.

**key differentiators**

* blends **design thinking** with technical depth—delivers delightful, consistent experiences without sacrificing performance.
* translates complex data from source systems into simple, trusted, and decision\-ready insights.
* proven **mentor/coach**, raising the bar for front‑end standards and code quality.
* mastery of **capacity \+ cost/perf trade‑offs** in fabric/power bi at scale.

**preferred certifications or equivalent demonstrated expertise**


certifications aren’t a proxy for capability, but they help validate depth in our stack.  

**strongly preferred**

* **microsoft certified: fabric analytics engineer associate (dp‑600\)**
* **microsoft certified: power bi data analyst associate (pl‑300\)**

**nice to have**

* **microsoft certified: azure data engineer associate (dp‑203\)**
* **microsoft certified: azure solutions architect expert (az‑305\)** or **azure administrator associate (az‑104\)**
* **power platform solution architect expert (pl‑600\)**
* recognized ux credentials (e.g., **nn/g ux certification**) or **iaap cpacc** for accessibility awareness

**location \& flexibility**

* **preferred**: twin cities, minnesota (st. paul hq) with a hybrid schedule
* **open to**: remote candidates with strong alignment to role requirements in one of johnson brothers established markets.

  

the expected pay range for this role is $77,316 \- $ 107,321\.19\. actual placement within range will be contingent upon several factors, including but not limited to the candidate's qualifications, education, experience, internal equity, union status (where applicable) and alignment with market data. johnson brothers offers eligible team members and their dependents comprehensive health benefits and programs, which include medical, dental, vision, life insurance, health savings and flexible spending accounts. other benefits for eligible team members include 401(k) with an employer match, short term disability coverage, company\-paid long term disability insurance, generous paid time off (pto) plans and paid parental leave.


johnson brothers is an equal opportunity employer, committed to fair treatment and hiring based on qualifications. we prohibit discrimination based on race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, military status, or any other protected status under applicable laws.

**worker sub\-type:**


regular**time type:**


full time

* create and maintain a **design system** for power bi (themes, layout patterns, typography, color, accessibility).
* standardize navigation (app structure, report landing pages, bookmarks) and **mobile‑first layouts**.
* build reusable visual templates and measure libraries to accelerate consistent delivery.

**performance optimization**

* own report performance: **dax optimization, model sizing, aggregation/hybrid tables, incremental refresh**, and visual/query reduction.
* monitor and tune **fabric/power bi capacity** using capacity metrics; set slis/slos for open time and interaction latency.
* establish performance test gates in ci/cd (performance analyzer baselines, dataset refresh thresholds).

**mentorship \& enablement**

* coach bi teams on dax patterns, modeling, and ux standards; run design and code reviews to ensure consistency across the organization.
* document standards, anti‑patterns, and “cookbook” guidance for repeatable delivery.

**quality \& governance**

* partner with data engineering to align **security (rls/ols), lineage, and metadata**.
* implement bi deployment processes including alm toolkit, git, and change management.

**technical stack \& tools**

* **core:** microsoft fabric, power bi desktop/service, dax, power query (m), **tabular editor,** **alm toolkit**, dax studio.
* **data/integration:** fabric pipelines \& dataflows gen2, azure sql/synapse, onelake, power bi deployment pipelines, azure devops.
* **monitoring \& ops:** power bi/ fabric **capacity metrics**, usage metrics, purview (lineage), azure monitor/log analytics.
* **ux/visualization:** power bi themes (json), custom visuals governance, accessibility guidelines (wcag), mobile layout optimizer.

**experience required**

* **8\+ years** in bi/analytics with **4\+ years** leading power bi front‑end architecture at enterprise scale.
* proven ability to collaborate with data engineering teams and a strong understanding of end\-to\-end data acquisition flows from source systems (e.g., erp, crm)
* demonstrated success consolidating disparate bi estates into a **unified fabric/power bi** platform.
* deep expertise in **dax performance tuning, model design, ux patterns, and capacity optimization**.
* hands‑on ci/cd for bi (git, deployment pipelines, alm toolkit) and production operations (refresh slas, incident handling).

**stakeholder interaction**

* translate executive and functional requirements into **task‑focused, decision‑ready** reporting.
* collaborate with data engineering, security, and governance to align modeling, lineage, and access.
* conduct design reviews, office hours, and enablement sessions; serve as a trusted advisor to business leaders.

**key differentiators**

* blends **design thinking** with technical depth—delivers delightful, consistent experiences without sacrificing performance.
* translates complex data from source systems into simple, trusted, and decision\-ready insights.
* proven **mentor/coach**, raising the bar for front‑end standards and code quality.
* mastery of **capacity \+ cost/perf trade‑offs** in fabric/power bi at scale.

**preferred certifications or equivalent demonstrated expertise**


certifications aren’t a proxy for capability, but they help validate depth in our stack.  

**strongly preferred**

* **microsoft certified: fabric analytics engineer associate (dp‑600\)**
* **microsoft certified: power bi data analyst associate (pl‑300\)**

**nice to have**

* **microsoft certified: azure data engineer associate (dp‑203\)**
* **microsoft certified: azure solutions architect expert (az‑305\)** or **azure administrator associate (az‑104\)**
* **power platform solution architect expert (pl‑600\)**
* recognized ux credentials (e.g., **nn/g ux certification**) or **iaap cpacc** for accessibility awareness

**location \& flexibility**

* **preferred**: twin cities, minnesota (st. paul hq) with a hybrid schedule
* **open to**: remote candidates with strong alignment to role requirements in one of johnson brothers established markets.

  

the expected pay range for this role is $77,316 \- $ 107,321\.19\. actual placement within range will be contingent upon several factors, including but not limited to the candidate's qualifications, education, experience, internal equity, union status (where applicable) and alignment with market data. johnson brothers offers eligible team members and their dependents comprehensive health benefits and programs, which include medical, dental, vision, life insurance, health savings and flexible spending accounts. other benefits for eligible team members include 401(k) with an employer match, short term disability coverage, company\-paid long term disability insurance, generous paid time off (pto) plans and paid parental leave.


johnson brothers is an equal opportunity employer, committed to fair treatment and hiring based on qualifications. we prohibit discrimination based on race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, military status, or any other protected status under applicable laws.

title: Junior Software Engineer — AI Operations & Platform Development
description:
**junior software engineer \| ai operations \& platform development** **company**

  

silverstay, inc. (odenton, md) — silverstay.com

 **reports to**

  

todd merrill, fractional cto

 **location**

  

remote *(united states)*

 **employment type**

  

full\-time

 **compensation**

  

$70,000 – $85,000 base, commensurate with experience

 **requisition date**

  

may 19, 2026

  

**about silverstay**
====================


at silverstay, we’re on a mission to be the leading healthcare services company nationwide for complex discharges by 2030\. our team provides solutions for medically and financially complex clients who are failing at home or transitioning out of the healthcare system. our guideposts of **empathy, creativity, collaboration, and ethical conduct**, while *“wowing”* our clients, mean that we’re not just providing solutions, we’re changing healthcare.  

  

our engineering team is building an ai\-powered hospital discharge management platform that replaces the spreadsheets and printed census tools used by case managers today. the platform ingests hospital data, applies ai to surface discharge barriers, assesses patient readiness, and recommends next steps across persona\-specific views for care navigation teams. we have multiple active healthcare partners, including adventist health and the university of maryland medical system, and are continually expanding. it is a hipaa\-regulated environment built on a serverless aws stack with a next.js front end.

**role summary**
================


we are hiring a junior software engineer to step into a dual\-focus role split roughly evenly between platform development and ai operations.  

  

on the ai automation side, they will inherit and expand a set of internal workflows, already in development, that use ai to eliminate repetitive, time\-consuming work for the internal team: generating call summaries, identifying facility matches, drafting referral and family communications, and producing contract patient summaries.  

  

on the development side, this person will take ownership of the silverstay platform codebase as the current tr development team completes its engagement and transitions the project out. this is engineering work: you'll be taking ownership of existing pipelines, extending them as new use cases emerge, and owning the end\-to\-end reliability of workflows the internal team depends on daily.


in practice, this role will start ai ops\-heavy. the platform development responsibilities will increase over time as the tr transition completes and the new hire builds familiarity with the codebase — we are not expecting day\-one platform ownership.


this is an ideal role for an early\-career engineer who wants broad ownership, direct mentorship from a fractional cto, and exposure to both modern cloud application development and applied ai operations in a regulated healthcare setting.

**key responsibilities**
========================

**platform development (\~50%)**
--------------------------------

* take over ongoing maintenance, bug fixes, and feature development of the silverstay platform following the tr development team's handoff.
* work across the stack: **next.js 14\+ / react / typescript** front end and **aws serverless** back end (api gateway, lambda, rds/postgresql, s3, cognito).
* deploy and maintain infrastructure using aws cdk, following established stack standards, tagging requirements, and deployment profiles.
* participate in code review, testing, and ci/cd workflows; keep the codebase healthy and well\-documented.
* contribute to the platform's pluggable analysis engine — adding and tuning ai analysis jobs as new hospital requirements emerge.

**ai operations \& implementation (\~50%)**
-------------------------------------------


silverstay's internal team handles a high volume of repetitive, time\-sensitive work; qualifying calls, facility placement matching, referral outreach, family communications, contract patient documentation. this half of the role is about building the internal ai\-powered workflows that take those tasks off the team's plate.

* take ownership of existing ai automation workflows — already built and in use — and maintain, improve, and extend them. active workflows include: generating structured call summaries from qualifying call recordings; identifying facility matches and auto\-drafting referral emails; agentic tracking of referral responses and generating family options emails; producing hospital\-specific patient summaries for contract cases where the hospital is the payer.
* iterate on and improve existing workflows as team needs evolve — improving output quality, reducing manual review, expanding to new use cases.
* build lightweight agentic workflows that can monitor inputs, take action, and loop back for review without requiring step\-by\-step human instruction.
* track ai usage and cost across internal workflows, optimize for prompt efficiency, and flag opportunities for caching or batching.
* document every workflow so the practice is repeatable, auditable, and not dependent on a single person.

**required qualifications**
===========================

* **0–3** years of professional software engineering experience (internships, co\-ops, and strong project work count).
* **hands\-on experience with aws** — comfortable working with core services such as lambda, api gateway, rds/postgresql, s3, or iam.
* **proficiency with next.js** and the react / typescript ecosystem.
* solid programming fundamentals in javascript/typescript, including version control with **git**.
* ability to work independently and take ownership of a codebase with guidance from a fractional cto rather than a large in\-house team.
* strong written communication — able to document work clearly for both technical and clinical stakeholders.
* comfort working in a regulated environment and following strict data\-handling rules (no phi in logs, least\-privilege access, audited actions).
* authorized to work in the united states.

**preferred qualifications**
============================

* exposure to hipaa, soc 2, or other regulated/compliance\-driven environments.
* experience with aws cdk or other infrastructure\-as\-code tooling.
* familiarity with ai/llm tooling — claude, the anthropic api, aws bedrock, or prompt engineering concepts.
* experience with serverless architectures and single\-table dynamodb design.
* interest in healthcare technology and improving outcomes for care teams and patients.
* experience inheriting and stabilizing a codebase from an outside development team.

**what success looks like (first 6 months)**
============================================

* ai automation workflows running cleanly and expanded with at least one new use case
* tr knowledge transfer complete, with no critical coverage gaps
* independent ownership of the deployment pipeline established
* at least one new hospital configuration or analysis job shipped end to end

**compensation \& logistics**
=============================

**base salary:** $70,000 – $85,000, commensurate with experience and skills.  

**location:** fully remote within the united states.  

**type:** full\-time. benefits to be discussed during the interview process.


silverstay is a 2024 baltimore business journal best places to work! learn more about silverstay here.

title: Sr. Sales Engineer
description:
### **about invoca:**



invoca is an ai\-powered revenue execution platform that brings together marketing, commerce, and contact center teams to turn every customer interaction into measurable, profitable growth. join our dynamic, fast\-growing team, where innovation and collaboration are at the core of our culture.


### **about the team:**



our sales engineering team is the technical heartbeat of the go\-to\-market organization. we partner with sales, product, and engineering to solve complex customer challenges. we are a collaborative, "egoless" team that thrives on continuous learning and pushing the boundaries of what ai can do for our customers.


### **what you'll do:**


### **architect the demo ecosystem**


* **end\-to\-end system design:** design and maintain the "gold standard" demo environment, including the system architecture, database schemas, and api layers that power invoca's technical storytelling.
* **full\-stack prototyping:** build functional user interfaces and dashboards that make complex ai processes "visible" and intuitive for non\-technical stakeholders.
* **scalable "scaffolding":** create modular demo assets and "click\-to\-deploy" sandboxes that the broader se team can easily customize for specific industry verticals.
* **process automation:** use toolsets such as **gumloop, zapier, or workato** to automate data flows between marketing touchpoints and sales outcomes.

### **lead technical strategy**


* **strategic pre\-sales:** partner with account executives on high\-value accounts to lead integration, technical discovery, and navigate complex technical blockers.
* **high\-stakes technical reviews:** act as the lead architect during deep\-dive technical reviews with customer ctos and security officers, defending the scalability, database integrity, and security of your proposed solutions.
* **ai thought leadership:** collaborate with product and engineering to ensure invoca remains "ai\-ready," acting as a feedback loop between the field and our core product developers.
* **governance \& safety:** implement "human\-in\-the\-loop" checkpoints to ensure ai\-generated demo content is reliable, safe, and free of hallucinations.

### **what you bring:**


* **business acumen:** the ability to translate a dry technical architecture into a "value story." you understand that the best code is useless if it doesn't solve a business pain point.
* **the "multiplier" mindset:** you enjoy building tools that make *other* people successful. you are obsessed with documentation and reusable code.
* **experience:** 5\+ years in sales engineering, solution architecture, or forward deployed engineering in a saas environment.
### **nice to have:**
* **full\-stack engineering dna:** you can design a multi\-tier system from the database schema up to the ui. proficiency in **python** or **javascript/node.js**.
* **architectural fluency:** deep understanding of restful apis, webhooks, and authentication protocols (oauth2\). you know how to connect disparate systems into a unified, automated workflow.
* **generative ai expertise:** practical experience with llm orchestration (rag, prompt engineering) and frameworks like langchain or llamaindex. you have built systems where ai *does* tasks via function calling/tool use.


you've read this far. if you're a smart, motivated, high\-integrity rock star who meets the above criteria, come join our team!


***location this is a remote\-first role. we are currently hiring in the following locations:***


***united states:*** *greater los angeles area (including santa barbara and san diego) · sf bay area · denver metro · austin metro · chicago metro · greater nyc area*


***candidates must be based within \~2 hour drive of these areas. occasional business travel may be required.***


### **salary, benefits \& perks:**



at invoca, all new hires in the u.s. receive benefits starting **on day one** of employment. our benefits offerings include:


*please note that benefits for teammates outside the u.s. may vary in accordance with their country's laws and regulations.*


* **flexible time off** – we encourage a healthy work\-life balance. our flexible paid time off policy allows you to recharge and take time away as needed.
* **paid holidays** – invoca provides **16 u.s. paid holidays**, including a winter break, giving you ample opportunity to refresh and spend time with friends and family.
* **health benefits** – our healthcare program includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, with multiple plan options so you can choose what works best for you and your family. **fertility assistance** is also included.
* **retirement** – invoca offers a 401(k) plan through fidelity with a company match of up to 4%.
* **stock options** – all employees are invited to share in invoca's success through stock options.
* **mental health program**– well\-being support on a broad range of issues is available through our springhealth program.
* **paid family leave** – up to 6 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for baby bonding, adoption, and caring for family members.
* **paid medical leave** – up to 12 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for childbirth and medical needs.
* **invacation** – as a thank\-you to our long\-term team members, we offer a bonus after 7 years of service.
* **wellness subsidy** – we provide a subsidy that can be applied toward gym memberships, fitness classes, and more.
* **position base range \-** salary range $130,000\-$160,000/year plus comission

### **dei statement**


*we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. we are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.*



\#li\-remote

*we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. we are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.*

title: Sr. Sales Engineer
description:
### **about invoca:**



invoca is an ai\-powered revenue execution platform that brings together marketing, commerce, and contact center teams to turn every customer interaction into measurable, profitable growth. join our dynamic, fast\-growing team, where innovation and collaboration are at the core of our culture.


### **about the team:**



our sales engineering team is the technical heartbeat of the go\-to\-market organization. we partner with sales, product, and engineering to solve complex customer challenges. we are a collaborative, "egoless" team that thrives on continuous learning and pushing the boundaries of what ai can do for our customers.


### **what you'll do:**


### **architect the demo ecosystem**


* **end\-to\-end system design:** design and maintain the "gold standard" demo environment, including the system architecture, database schemas, and api layers that power invoca's technical storytelling.
* **full\-stack prototyping:** build functional user interfaces and dashboards that make complex ai processes "visible" and intuitive for non\-technical stakeholders.
* **scalable "scaffolding":** create modular demo assets and "click\-to\-deploy" sandboxes that the broader se team can easily customize for specific industry verticals.
* **process automation:** use toolsets such as **gumloop, zapier, or workato** to automate data flows between marketing touchpoints and sales outcomes.

### **lead technical strategy**


* **strategic pre\-sales:** partner with account executives on high\-value accounts to lead integration, technical discovery, and navigate complex technical blockers.
* **high\-stakes technical reviews:** act as the lead architect during deep\-dive technical reviews with customer ctos and security officers, defending the scalability, database integrity, and security of your proposed solutions.
* **ai thought leadership:** collaborate with product and engineering to ensure invoca remains "ai\-ready," acting as a feedback loop between the field and our core product developers.
* **governance \& safety:** implement "human\-in\-the\-loop" checkpoints to ensure ai\-generated demo content is reliable, safe, and free of hallucinations.

### **what you bring:**


* **business acumen:** the ability to translate a dry technical architecture into a "value story." you understand that the best code is useless if it doesn't solve a business pain point.
* **the "multiplier" mindset:** you enjoy building tools that make *other* people successful. you are obsessed with documentation and reusable code.
* **experience:** 5\+ years in sales engineering, solution architecture, or forward deployed engineering in a saas environment.
### **nice to have:**
* **full\-stack engineering dna:** you can design a multi\-tier system from the database schema up to the ui. proficiency in **python** or **javascript/node.js**.
* **architectural fluency:** deep understanding of restful apis, webhooks, and authentication protocols (oauth2\). you know how to connect disparate systems into a unified, automated workflow.
* **generative ai expertise:** practical experience with llm orchestration (rag, prompt engineering) and frameworks like langchain or llamaindex. you have built systems where ai *does* tasks via function calling/tool use.


you've read this far. if you're a smart, motivated, high\-integrity rock star who meets the above criteria, come join our team!


***location this is a remote\-first role. we are currently hiring in the following locations:***


***united states:*** *greater los angeles area (including santa barbara and san diego) · sf bay area · denver metro · austin metro · chicago metro · greater nyc area*


***candidates must be based within \~2 hour drive of these areas. occasional business travel may be required.***


### **salary, benefits \& perks:**



at invoca, all new hires in the u.s. receive benefits starting **on day one** of employment. our benefits offerings include:


*please note that benefits for teammates outside the u.s. may vary in accordance with their country's laws and regulations.*


* **flexible time off** – we encourage a healthy work\-life balance. our flexible paid time off policy allows you to recharge and take time away as needed.
* **paid holidays** – invoca provides **16 u.s. paid holidays**, including a winter break, giving you ample opportunity to refresh and spend time with friends and family.
* **health benefits** – our healthcare program includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, with multiple plan options so you can choose what works best for you and your family. **fertility assistance** is also included.
* **retirement** – invoca offers a 401(k) plan through fidelity with a company match of up to 4%.
* **stock options** – all employees are invited to share in invoca's success through stock options.
* **mental health program**– well\-being support on a broad range of issues is available through our springhealth program.
* **paid family leave** – up to 6 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for baby bonding, adoption, and caring for family members.
* **paid medical leave** – up to 12 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for childbirth and medical needs.
* **invacation** – as a thank\-you to our long\-term team members, we offer a bonus after 7 years of service.
* **wellness subsidy** – we provide a subsidy that can be applied toward gym memberships, fitness classes, and more.
* **position base range \-** salary range $130,000\-$160,000/year plus comission

### **dei statement**


*we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. we are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.*



\#li\-remote

title: Customer Engineer, Data & Analytics Req#112
description:
**about premier cloud**


premier cloud is a google cloud premier partner dedicated to helping organizations modernize, collaborate, and scale through the power of cloud technology. founded in 2001, we deliver cloud consulting, managed services, and tailored solutions to smb and enterprise clients across north america.

  

as one of canada’s fastest\-growing companies, we help businesses unlock the full potential of google cloud, along with leading saas platforms like jumpcloud, lumin, and virtru. our team is driven by innovation, technical excellence, and a shared commitment to delivering measurable impact for our clients.

  

at premier cloud, we take pride in maintaining a collaborative, light\-hearted culture that values creativity, growth, and inclusion. year after year, we’re recognized as a great place to work\-certified™ organization and featured among the best workplaces in technology.

 **about the role**


join us as a **customer engineer, data \& analytics**


premier cloud \- austin, tx

**premier cloud at a glance**

* premier cloud, a google cloud premier partner, specializes in helping smb and enterprise clients across north america modernize, collaborate, and innovate using google cloud and saas solutions. they offer consulting, managed services, and cloud solutions to major organizations.
* one of the fastest\-growing companies in canada, headquartered in victoria, bc, with an office in austin, tx.
* certified a great place to work for 6 years in a row
* premier cloud’s professional services include:
* + licensing and migration services for businesses wanting to implement google workspace
	+ data, ai, and infrastructure solutions powered by google cloud
	+ consulting, training, and various professional services surrounding google cloud’s products.


as a **google cloud customer engineer, data \& analytics** at **premier cloud**, you'll be the technical expert in client\-facing roles, focusing on scoping, designing, and delivering data solutions on google cloud platform (gcp). you'll work with technical teams to showcase google cloud's benefits and help clients leverage gcp for their data needs. your goal is to turn complex challenges into scalable, high\-performance data architectures that enable insights, decision\-making, and innovation. you'll explain technical features, assist with architectural design, and resolve issues for prospective customers. collaborating with clients, cloud architects, and sales teams, you'll deliver solutions aligned with business goals. google cloud enables organizations to digitally transform using advanced technology and tools for sustainable growth.

#### **responsibilities**

#### **solution delivery \& client partnership**

* **lead complex technical discovery** and needs analysis with clients and sales teams to deeply understand and solve their unique data challenges and goals.
* **design and implement** scalable, high\-performance data solutions on gcp, directly influencing client success and transformation.
* **develop, build, and deploy** critical data pipelines, data warehouses, and data lakes using core gcp services (bigquery, dataflow, dataproc, etc.).
* **leverage your expertise to optimize** data architectures for cost\-efficiency and maximum performance.

#### **data engineering \& implementation**

* build and maintain etl/elt pipelines to ingest, transform, and load data from various sources.
* ensure data quality, integrity, and security throughout the data lifecycle.
* work with cloud architects and developers to integrate data solutions into end\-to\-end client applications.

#### **client success \& consulting**

* conduct client readiness assessments to leverage google cloud for data analysis.
* provide expert technical guidance and consulting support to clients throughout project lifecycles.
* contribute to the development of internal best practices, knowledge sharing, and reusable solution templates for presales use.

#### **qualifications**

* **minimum qualifications**
* + proven experience in designing, building, and deploying data solutions on gcp.
	+ strong expertise in sql, data warehouse design, and data pipeline development.
	+ understanding of cloud architecture principles and best practices.
	+ excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders
* **preferred qualifications**
* + 3\+ years of experience in a data engineering role.
	+ experience with looker or other data visualization tools.
	+ knowledge of ai/ml technologies and their application in data solutions.
	+ **professional data engineer** or other relevant gcp certifications.

#### **why premier cloud? your impact and growth**


join a *great place to work* (certified for 6 consecutive years) where your expertise is valued and actively developed.

* **direct impact:** you will be pivotal in turning complex client challenges into scalable, high\-performance data architectures that drive business insights and innovation.
* **elite collaboration:** work directly with google teams on strategic analytics initiatives, positioning yourself at the forefront of cloud innovation.
* **career investment:** we invest heavily in your growth, offering ongoing training, certifications, and opportunities to lead cutting\-edge google cloud projects.
* **culture of trust:** thrive in a culture of trust and autonomy, supported by flexible remote work options and premium equipment.

 **benefits**

* competitive compensation: a package that reflects your experience and impact.
* growth \& development: ongoing training, certifications, and opportunities to work on cutting\-edge google cloud projects.
* collaboration with google: work directly with google teams on strategic analytics initiatives.
* comprehensive benefits: health coverage, paid time off, and relocation assistance (if applicable).
* work environment: flexible remote work with premium equipment and a culture of trust and autonomy.

title: FinTech - AI Finance - Front-end Software Developer - Senior
description:
location: chicago
other locations: anywhere in region
salary: competitive
date: may 19, 2026
**job description**
-------------------


requisition id: 1711458
location: chicago, akron, arlington, atlanta, austin, baltimore, birmingham, boca raton, boston, buffalo, charleston, charlotte, chattanooga, cincinnati, cleveland, columbus, dallas, denver, des moines, detroit, fort worth, grand rapids, greenville, hartford, hoboken, honolulu, houston, indianapolis, irvine, jacksonville, kansas, los angeles, las vegas, louisville, mclean, memphis, miami, milwaukee, minneapolis, nashville, new orleans, new york, oklahoma, orlando, palo alto, philadelphia, phoenix, pittsburgh, portland, providence, raleigh, richmond, rochester, rogers, sacramento, salt lake city, san antonio, san diego, san francisco, san jose, seattle, secaucus, stamford, st. louis, tallahassee, tampa, toledo, tucson, tulsa, washington, westlake village

  



at ey, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

  



we’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. join ey and help to build a better working world.

 **fintech – ai finance – front\-end software engineer \- senior**

  



ey.ai finance sits at the intersection of finance transformation, enterprise consulting, and product engineering. we're building a generative ai application purpose\-built for financial planning \& analysis — think variance analysis in minutes instead of weeks, predictive forecasting grounded in rigorous ml, and scenario planning powered by a custom multi\-agent architecture.

  


**the opportunity**



this isn't a typical consulting engagement. we're building a centrally managed software asset designed to scale across sectors and clients. you'd be joining a small, high\-velocity product team responsible for the full ui surface of that product — from interactive analytics and chat\-driven workflows to config\-driven dashboards built for both cfos and fp\&a analysts.

  

in this role, you will interact with business stakeholders to evaluate business models, processes, and operations. you will gather, understand, and analyze business requirements, translating them into technical specifications. your expertise in specific technology platforms will be crucial in providing in\-depth analysis related to implementation, customization, and optimization.

  


**your key responsibilities**



we're looking for a front\-end software engineer to take ownership of the user interfaces for our ai\-powered finance application. you'll play a key role in translating design into modern, performant interfaces, shaping the user experience, and helping establish the front\-end architecture as we scale from proof\-of\-concept to production saas.

  



as a senior platform business analyst, you will play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. you will spend your time engaging with clients, analyzing their requirements, and collaborating with technical teams to design and deliver effective system architecture solutions. this role presents exciting challenges and opportunities for growth, as you will help clients maximize their investment in technology platforms through effective use and continuous improvement. regular travel may be required to meet with clients and participate in project activities.


* design, develop, and maintain responsive uis using **react / next.js** and **typescript**
* translate product and ux designs (figma) into clean, functional, and scalable interfaces aligned with ey brand standards
* build and refine interactive components — including chat interfaces, data visualizations, and configuration\-driven views
* collaborate closely with backend engineers, data scientists, and product leadership to deliver cohesive features end\-to\-end
* contribute to component libraries, internal documentation, and knowledge transfer across distributed teams
* engage with business stakeholders to gather and analyze business requirements.
* collaborate with technical teams to design and deliver system architecture solutions.
* tailor technology platforms to align with business processes and objectives.

  


**skills and attributes for success**



to thrive in this role, you will need a blend of technical and interpersonal skills. your ability to build relationships and communicate effectively will be key to your success. additionally, your analytical mindset will enable you to solve complex problems and drive outcomes.


* strong understanding of technology platform methodologies and system configuration design.
* proficiency in technology business architecture frameworks and requirements definition.
* experience in conducting technology cost\-benefit analysis and delivering training.
* ability to manage client relationships and communicate with impact.

  


**to qualify for the role, you must have**


* bachelor’s degree required (4\-year degree).
* 3–5 years of professional frontend development experience
* proficiency in **react / next.js** and **typescript**
* solid experience with **tailwind css**
* strong understanding of frontend performance optimization, accessibility, and responsive design
* experience integrating with **restful apis** and working alongside backend services
* ability to work independently and own feature development from design handoff to deployment
* demonstrated portfolio or code samples showing polished, production\-quality ui work
* strong technical skills in application functional design and platform methodologies.
* expertise in system configuration design and technology business architecture frameworks.
* proficiency in defining and analyzing technology business requirements.
* experience in conducting technology cost\-benefit analyses and delivering training.
* exceptional relationship\-building skills to foster trust with clients and colleagues.
* strong digital fluency and emotional agility in a fast\-paced environment.
* proven problem\-solving abilities and critical thinking skills.
* a commitment to continuous learning and professional development.

  


**ideally, you’ll also have**


* experience building **custom chat interfaces** or highly interactive, stateful web applications
* exposure to **data visualization libraries** (e.g., chart.js, d3\.js, recharts)
* familiarity with **azure web apps** and/or python\-based backend environments
* interest in finance, fp\&a workflows, or enterprise saas — domain experience is a plus, not a requirement
* experience with agile methodologies and project management.
* familiarity with data analysis tools and techniques.
* knowledge of industry trends and best practices in technology implementation.

  


**what we look for**



we seek individuals who are not only skilled but also possess a passion for technology and a commitment to delivering exceptional client service. top performers demonstrate strong analytical abilities, creativity in problem\-solving, and the capacity to collaborate effectively with diverse teams.

  

**what we offer you**  

at ey, we’ll develop you with future\-focused skills and equip you with world\-class experiences. we’ll empower you in a flexible environment, and fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. learn more.


* we offer a comprehensive compensation and benefits package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to the business. the base salary range for this job in all geographic locations in the us is $102,500 to $187,900\. the base salary range for new york city metro area, washington state and california (excluding sacramento) is $122,900 to $213,400\. individual salaries within those ranges are determined through a wide variety of factors including but not limited to education, experience, knowledge, skills and geography. in addition, our total rewards package includes medical and dental coverage, pension and 401(k) plans, and a wide range of paid time off options.
* join us in our team\-led and leader\-enabled hybrid model. our expectation is for most people in external, client serving roles to work together in person 40\-60% of the time over the course of an engagement, project or year.
* under our flexible vacation policy, you’ll decide how much vacation time you need based on your own personal circumstances. you’ll also be granted time off for designated ey paid holidays, winter/summer breaks, personal/family care, and other leaves of absence when needed to support your physical, financial, and emotional well\-being.

  


**are you ready to shape your future with confidence? apply today.**  

ey accepts applications for this position on an on\-going basis.

  



for those living in california, please click here for additional information.

  



ey focuses on high\-ethical standards and integrity among its employees and expects all candidates to demonstrate these qualities.

  


**ey \| building a better working world**

  



ey is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

  



enabled by data, ai and advanced technology, ey teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

  



ey teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi\-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, ey teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

  



ey provides equal employment opportunities to applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, pregnancy, genetic information, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other legally protected basis, including arrest and conviction records, in accordance with applicable law.

  



ey is committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities including veterans with disabilities. if you have a disability and either need assistance applying online or need to request an accommodation during any part of the application process, please call 1\-800\-ey\-help3, select option 2 for candidate related inquiries, then select option 1 for candidate queries and finally select option 2 for candidates with an inquiry which will route you to ey’s talent shared services team (tss) or email the tss at ssc.customersupport@ey.com.

  

  

**nearest major market:** chicago

title: Applied AI Engineer
description:
**about quizlet:**  

at quizlet, our mission is to help every learner achieve their outcomes in the most effective and delightful way. we’re a $1b\+ learning platform used by two\-thirds of u.s. high school students and half of college students, powering over 1 billion learning interactions each week.  

we blend cognitive science with machine learning to personalize and enhance the learning experience for students, professionals, and lifelong learners alike. we’re energized by the potential to power more learners through multiple approaches and various tools. **let’s build the future of learning**
join us to design and deliver ai\-powered learning tools that scale across the world and unlock human potential.  

**about the team (applied ai):**
--------------------------------



our mission is to invent and deploy the next generation of intelligent, personalized, and adaptive learning experiences. we’re consolidating ai efforts across the company into a unified portfolio and are accountable for a disproportionate share of quizlet’s growth and product differentiation. you’ll partner closely with product, data science, and the ai \& data platform to deliver an ai‑driven learning coach that’s recognized as best‑in‑class.


**about the role:**
-------------------



we are looking for applied ai engineers at staff and sr. staff levels (note: leveling decisions will be made during the interview process).


you’ll be working at the forefront of our ai strategy, shaping quizlet’s ai development in one of the two complementary domains:


**personalization \& ranking** – retrieval and ranking systems that match learners with the right content, experiences, and monetization moments across surfaces (search, feed, notifications, ads).


**generative ai \& agentic systems** – llm‑powered tutoring, content understanding/synthesis, and tools that boost learner outcomes and creator productivity.



you will work on a variety of models and modeling systems (from two‑tower retrieval and multi‑task rankers to rag/llm pipelines), ensure robust evaluation, and responsible deployment


we’re happy to share that this is an **onsite position** in either our denver, san francisco, seattle, or nyc. to help foster team collaboration, we require that employees be in the office a **minimum of three days per week:** monday, wednesday, and thursday and as needed by your manager or the company. we believe that this working environment facilitates increased work efficiency, team partnership, and supports growth as an employee and organization.
### **in this role, you will:**


* contribute to the technical roadmap for applied ai across personalization, ranking, search, recommendations, and genai/llm systems; help connect modeling work to business metrics (engaged learners, conversion, retention, revenue)
* build components of end‑to‑end ml systems: candidate sourcing, embedding platforms \& ann retrieval, multi‑stage ranking (early/late), and value modeling with guardrails for fairness and integrity
* implement llm‑based features: build rag pipelines, apply instruction‑/preference‑tuning techniques (e.g., sft/dpo), optimize prompts, and improve latency/cost‑aware inference; contribute to offline evals \+ human‑in‑the‑loop and online success metrics
* help develop "learner 360" representations by working with behavior signals, explicit inputs, and conversational context to create robust embeddings reused across surfaces
* support evaluation infrastructure: contribute to the eval harness for both ranking and generative systems (offline metrics like ndcg/auc/bleu/bertscore; quality/safety scorecards), and help close the loop with online a/b experiments
* ship reliable systems at scale: ensure training‑serving consistency, implement drift detection, follow canarying/rollback protocols, participate in on‑call rotation for model services, and maintain strong ci/cd for features \& models
* collaborate with and learn from senior ml/swe teammates; write high‑quality code and follow best practices for experimentation rigor and reproducibility
* work closely with product, design, legal, and data science on objectives, tradeoffs, and responsible ai practices
* stay current with ml research (recsys, llms, multimodal) and propose new methods that could improve learner outcomes
### **what you bring to the table:**


* 6\+ years of industry experience in applied ml/ai or ml‑heavy software engineering
* bs/ms in cs, ml, or related quantitative field (or equivalent experience)
* experience building ranking/personalization or search systems (retrieval, two‑tower/dual encoders, multi‑task rankers) and contributing to online metric improvements (e.g., ctr, session depth, retention)
* hands‑on experience with llm/genai systems: data curation, fine‑tuning (sft/peft, preference optimization), prompt engineering, evaluation, and productionization considerations (latency/cost/safety)
* strong skills in python/pytorch, data and feature engineering, distributed training/inference on gpus, and familiarity with modern mlops (model registry, feature stores, monitoring, drift)
* solid experiment design (offline/online), metrics literacy, and ability to translate product goals into modeling solutions
* strong collaboration skills and eagerness to learn from senior engineers; some experience mentoring junior teammates is a plus
### **bonus points if you have:**


* edtech or consumer mobile experience; conversational tutoring or learning science‑informed modeling
* publications/open‑source with recsys/llms (e.g., recsys, kdd, neurips, iclr, acl), or contributions to safety/guardrails tooling
* experience building on a modern mlops stack (feature mgmt, orchestration, streaming, online inference at scale)
### **compensation, benefits \& perks:**


* quizlet is an equal opportunity employer. we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. salary transparency helps to mitigate unfair hiring practices when it comes to discrimination and pay gaps. total compensation for this role is market competitive, including a starting base salary of $178,000 \- $316,000 depending on location and experience, as well as company stock options
* collaborate with your manager and team to create a healthy work\-life balance
* 20 vacation days that we expect you to take!
* competitive health, dental, and vision insurance (100% employee and 75% dependent ppo, dental, vsp choice)
* employer\-sponsored 401k plan with company match
* access to linkedin learning and other resources to support professional growth
* paid family leave, fsa, hsa, commuter benefits, and wellness benefits
* 40 hours of annual paid time off to participate in volunteer programs of choice
**why join quizlet?**  

 \\ud83c\\udf0e massive reach: 60m\+ users, 1b\+ interactions per week
 \\ud83e\\udde0 cutting\-edge tech: generative ai, adaptive learning, cognitive science
 \\ud83d\\udcc8 strong momentum: top\-tier investors, sustainable business, real traction
 \\ud83c\\udfaf mission\-first: work that makes a difference in people’s lives
 \\ud83e\\udd1d inclusive culture: committed to equity, diversity, and belonging ***we strive to make everyone feel comfortable and welcome!***
we work to create a holistic interview process, where both quizlet and candidates have an opportunity to view what it would be like to work together, in exploring a mutually beneficial partnership.  

we provide a transparent setting that gives a comprehensive view of who we are!  

  

**in closing:**  

at quizlet, we’re excited about passionate people joining our team—even if you don’t check every box on the requirements list. we value unique perspectives and believe everyone has something meaningful to contribute. our culture is all about taking initiative, learning through challenges, and striving for high\-quality work while staying curious and open to new ideas. we believe in honest, respectful communication, thoughtful collaboration, and creating a supportive space where everyone can grow and succeed together.”  

quizlet’s success as an online learning community depends on a strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion.  

as an equal opportunity employer and a tech company committed to societal change, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds. women, people of color, members of the lgbtq\+ community, individuals with disabilities, and veterans are strongly encouraged to apply. come join us! **to all recruiters and placement agencies:**  

at this time, quizlet does not accept unsolicited agency resumes and/or profiles.
please do not forward unsolicited agency resumes to our website or to any quizlet employee. quizlet will not pay fees to any third\-party agency or firm nor will it be responsible for any agency fees associated with unsolicited resumes. all unsolicited resumes received will be considered the property of quizlet.
\#li\-onsite

we may use artificial intelligence (ai) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. these tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. if you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Applied AI Engineer
description:
**about quizlet:**  

at quizlet, our mission is to help every learner achieve their outcomes in the most effective and delightful way. we’re a $1b\+ learning platform used by two\-thirds of u.s. high school students and half of college students, powering over 1 billion learning interactions each week.  

we blend cognitive science with machine learning to personalize and enhance the learning experience for students, professionals, and lifelong learners alike. we’re energized by the potential to power more learners through multiple approaches and various tools. **let’s build the future of learning**
join us to design and deliver ai\-powered learning tools that scale across the world and unlock human potential.  

**about the team (applied ai):**
--------------------------------



our mission is to invent and deploy the next generation of intelligent, personalized, and adaptive learning experiences. we’re consolidating ai efforts across the company into a unified portfolio and are accountable for a disproportionate share of quizlet’s growth and product differentiation. you’ll partner closely with product, data science, and the ai \& data platform to deliver an ai‑driven learning coach that’s recognized as best‑in‑class.


**about the role:**
-------------------



we are looking for applied ai engineers at staff and sr. staff levels (note: leveling decisions will be made during the interview process).


you’ll be working at the forefront of our ai strategy, shaping quizlet’s ai development in one of the two complementary domains:


**personalization \& ranking** – retrieval and ranking systems that match learners with the right content, experiences, and monetization moments across surfaces (search, feed, notifications, ads).


**generative ai \& agentic systems** – llm‑powered tutoring, content understanding/synthesis, and tools that boost learner outcomes and creator productivity.



you will work on a variety of models and modeling systems (from two‑tower retrieval and multi‑task rankers to rag/llm pipelines), ensure robust evaluation, and responsible deployment


we’re happy to share that this is an **onsite position** in either our denver, san francisco, seattle, or nyc. to help foster team collaboration, we require that employees be in the office a **minimum of three days per week:** monday, wednesday, and thursday and as needed by your manager or the company. we believe that this working environment facilitates increased work efficiency, team partnership, and supports growth as an employee and organization.
### **in this role, you will:**


* contribute to the technical roadmap for applied ai across personalization, ranking, search, recommendations, and genai/llm systems; help connect modeling work to business metrics (engaged learners, conversion, retention, revenue)
* build components of end‑to‑end ml systems: candidate sourcing, embedding platforms \& ann retrieval, multi‑stage ranking (early/late), and value modeling with guardrails for fairness and integrity
* implement llm‑based features: build rag pipelines, apply instruction‑/preference‑tuning techniques (e.g., sft/dpo), optimize prompts, and improve latency/cost‑aware inference; contribute to offline evals \+ human‑in‑the‑loop and online success metrics
* help develop "learner 360" representations by working with behavior signals, explicit inputs, and conversational context to create robust embeddings reused across surfaces
* support evaluation infrastructure: contribute to the eval harness for both ranking and generative systems (offline metrics like ndcg/auc/bleu/bertscore; quality/safety scorecards), and help close the loop with online a/b experiments
* ship reliable systems at scale: ensure training‑serving consistency, implement drift detection, follow canarying/rollback protocols, participate in on‑call rotation for model services, and maintain strong ci/cd for features \& models
* collaborate with and learn from senior ml/swe teammates; write high‑quality code and follow best practices for experimentation rigor and reproducibility
* work closely with product, design, legal, and data science on objectives, tradeoffs, and responsible ai practices
* stay current with ml research (recsys, llms, multimodal) and propose new methods that could improve learner outcomes
### **what you bring to the table:**


* 6\+ years of industry experience in applied ml/ai or ml‑heavy software engineering
* bs/ms in cs, ml, or related quantitative field (or equivalent experience)
* experience building ranking/personalization or search systems (retrieval, two‑tower/dual encoders, multi‑task rankers) and contributing to online metric improvements (e.g., ctr, session depth, retention)
* hands‑on experience with llm/genai systems: data curation, fine‑tuning (sft/peft, preference optimization), prompt engineering, evaluation, and productionization considerations (latency/cost/safety)
* strong skills in python/pytorch, data and feature engineering, distributed training/inference on gpus, and familiarity with modern mlops (model registry, feature stores, monitoring, drift)
* solid experiment design (offline/online), metrics literacy, and ability to translate product goals into modeling solutions
* strong collaboration skills and eagerness to learn from senior engineers; some experience mentoring junior teammates is a plus
### **bonus points if you have:**


* edtech or consumer mobile experience; conversational tutoring or learning science‑informed modeling
* publications/open‑source with recsys/llms (e.g., recsys, kdd, neurips, iclr, acl), or contributions to safety/guardrails tooling
* experience building on a modern mlops stack (feature mgmt, orchestration, streaming, online inference at scale)
### **compensation, benefits \& perks:**


* quizlet is an equal opportunity employer. we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. salary transparency helps to mitigate unfair hiring practices when it comes to discrimination and pay gaps. total compensation for this role is market competitive, including a starting base salary of $178,000 \- $316,000 depending on location and experience, as well as company stock options
* collaborate with your manager and team to create a healthy work\-life balance
* 20 vacation days that we expect you to take!
* competitive health, dental, and vision insurance (100% employee and 75% dependent ppo, dental, vsp choice)
* employer\-sponsored 401k plan with company match
* access to linkedin learning and other resources to support professional growth
* paid family leave, fsa, hsa, commuter benefits, and wellness benefits
* 40 hours of annual paid time off to participate in volunteer programs of choice
**why join quizlet?**  

 \\ud83c\\udf0e massive reach: 60m\+ users, 1b\+ interactions per week
 \\ud83e\\udde0 cutting\-edge tech: generative ai, adaptive learning, cognitive science
 \\ud83d\\udcc8 strong momentum: top\-tier investors, sustainable business, real traction
 \\ud83c\\udfaf mission\-first: work that makes a difference in people’s lives
 \\ud83e\\udd1d inclusive culture: committed to equity, diversity, and belonging ***we strive to make everyone feel comfortable and welcome!***
